criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Correct Statutory Provisions Cited
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output cites Section 206(4) of the Investment Advisers Act as the statutory provision violated in connection with the cherry-picking trade allocation allegation (in the 'WCA's trade allocation practices' section), not Sections 206(1) or 206(2). While Sections 206(1) and 206(2) are mentioned elsewhere in the document (in connection with brokerage arrangements and Bellini's directed brokerage conduct), they are not cited as the statutory provisions violated in connection with the cherry-picking allegation itself. The criterion requires that 206(1) and/or 206(2) be identified specifically for the cherry-picking allegation.
